title: Boat Rental in Thousand Islands: A Complete Cost Guide for 2026
html:
Hiring a boat in the Thousand Islands chain on Lake Ontario and the St. Lawrence River offers a unique way to explore the archipelago, with prices typically ranging from €250 for a half-day small motorboat to over €2,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. Whether you depart from Gananoque or Clayton, you can compare verified options on BoatHire24 to find the perfect vessel for your trip.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Thousand Islands?

A half-day rental of a small motorboat starts at around €250, while a full-day catamaran can cost from €1,200. Here is a typical price breakdown for 2026:

  • Shared tour on a pontoon boat: from €120 per person for an hour, including a guide to Boldt Castle
  • Private small motorboat (16-20ft) half-day (4h): from €250, suitable for up to 5 guests from Kingston or Alexandria Bay
  • Catamaran full-day (8h): from €1,200, sleeping up to 12 passengers, popular for sunset cruises from Clayton
  • Crewed luxury yacht (30ft+) full-day: from €2,000, including a captain for a custom itinerary to the Rock Island Lighthouse

Which boat to rent in the Thousand Islands?

The choice depends on your group size and whether you want to explore independently or with a skipper. For those preferring a self-drive experience, many smaller boats do not require a licence; operators confirm requirements at booking. You can also book a boat online to compare models and prices.

  1. Motorboat (16-20ft): ideal for a couple or small family to explore the inner islands; capacity 4-6; from €250 half-day
  2. Limpopo Axopar 28ft: a stylish crossover with a cabin for overnighting; capacity 8; from €600 half-day, see the the Limpopo for hire listing for details
  3. Sloop or sailing yacht (30-40ft): for those who prefer sailing on Lake Ontario; capacity 6-10; from €1,500 full-day
  4. Catamaran (35-45ft): extra deck space for entertaining; capacity 12; from €1,200 full-day
  5. Crewed motor yacht (40ft+): includes captain and often a host; capacity 8-12; from €2,500 full-day

Where to set off in the Thousand Islands

There are several well-equipped marinas and departure points. Gananoque, Ontario, is a popular gateway with the Gananoque Marina offering secure parking and direct access. In New York state, Clayton’s French Creek Marina provides easy launch into the river, while Alexandria Bay has the Otter Creek Marina. Boldt Castle is a common destination, roughly 3 nautical miles from Gananoque. For those exploring the Canadian side, Ivy Lea Marina and the town of Rockport are excellent bases for a day trip.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Thousand Islands?

In Canada, a pleasure craft operator card is legally required for motorised boats, while in US waters a boater education card is needed for those born after 1983. Rental operators will confirm exact requirements, and many permit experienced skippers without a formal licence if they pass a brief safety check. For sailing vessels, no licence is typically demanded, but a sailing proficiency test may be given. If you prefer not to drive, you can hire a captain for an additional fee. To understand the booking process, read how boat rental works on the platform.

Local tip: book your trip in July or August to enjoy the warmest water temperatures and avoid the spring currents that can be tricky near the Wellesley Island narrows.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €2,500
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Typical trip length: 4 to 8 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in the Thousand Islands

Can I rent a boat for a single day in the Thousand Islands?

Yes, half-day and full-day rentals are widely available from marinas in Gananoque, Alexandria Bay, and Clayton, with prices starting at €250 for a half-day self-drive motorboat.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in the Thousand Islands?

The peak season runs from late June to early September, with July and August offering the most reliable weather and calmest conditions on Lake Ontario and the St. Lawrence River.

Are there guided tours available for first-time visitors?

Yes, many operators offer captain-guided tours that include Boldt Castle, Singer Castle, and the Rock Island Lighthouse, with shared tours from €120 per person.

Can I moor at the islands when renting a private boat?

Yes, public docking facilities are available at Boldt Castle and several state parks, though some US islands require a day-use permit that the rental operator can advise on.

To explore the full range of options for your 2026 trip, check availability on our platform. Boat owners are welcome to list their own vessels and earn income from the thousands of visitors seeking authentic boating experiences in the Thousand Islands.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Thousand Islands and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
